segs.utf8:
Hello and welcome
to Real Easy English.
In this podcast,

we have real conversations
in easy English to help you learn.
I'm Beth.
And I'm Neil,

and you can find a video version
of this podcast on our website,
bbclearningenglish.com.

where you can also find
a free worksheet.
How was your weekend, Neil?
My weekend was very good.
I did lots of fun things.
Oh, good. What about you?

Yeah, I went to a museum that was not
that interesting.

And I did some other
interesting things too.

Well, today,
Beth, we are talking about days out

and places that are good to visit
for a day trip.

Very nice.
Let's get started then.

So tell us about your boring day,
by the sound of it.
So it was a prison museum
about, like, medieval England.

So I feel like it could have been
a bit more exciting,

but instead I was reading a lot
about religion and kings and stuff.

OK, so you spent your weekend learning
about prisoners from the Middle Ages?
Yeah.
Yeah, OK.

I wouldn't recommend it too much,
but museums can be a good...
Yeah.
...thing.

What about a day trip
that you've enjoyed?

I used to go on lots of day trips
with my kids.
Mm, yeah.
So, fun things to do,
we'd go to the seaside in the summer.

But they don't really want
to hang around with me anymore.
So. You're not cool.
I'm on my own. Yeah, so
I like watching sport.

So I might meet friends
and go and watch sport.

On Friday, I spent most of the day
watching a cricket match.

Nice. That sounds
like a good day out.

Yeah. Well, actually, I did go
to a museum, but, I also went

to the theatre this weekend and
that was a really enjoyable day trip.
Did you do any sightseeing?

Well, yeah, we walked past
quite a lot of famous sights,
so we went past Big Ben.

We walked past the London Eye,
Trafalgar Square.
Yeah, we did a lot of walking.
I'm quite tired, actually.
And are you a thrill seeker?

So do you like really adventurous
day trips?

Like, do you like parachuting or
bungee jumping or any of those things
or roller coasters?
Theme parks?
I would say no, not really.

I think if I'm on holiday,
I'm a bit more adventurous.

So I've done things like,
you know, been in speedboats

and that kind of stuff,
which I find quite enjoyable.
If I'm, if I'm abroad it tends to be.

But I don't really like roller
coasters and that sort of thing

because they, they scare me
and make me feel a bit sick.

OK. So I wouldn't go
to a theme park for a day out.

What about you?
Are you a thrill seeker?

Not anymore. I used to like
theme parks when I was younger,

but I don't really like them anymore.
OK, Beth.

So is there anywhere that you have
always wanted to visit for a day trip,
but you haven't?
There is actually. So, Brighton.

I went to Brighton
when I was a child,

but I can't remember it at all,
to be honest.

So I'd really like to go there on
a day trip because it's not too far.

And I like the beach
and I want to have an ice cream.
Maybe some fish and chips.
I used to live in Brighton.
Did you?
Yeah.

You can take me on a day trip
and show me around.
I definitely think you should visit.
How about you, Neil?

Is there anywhere that you've
always wanted to go on a day trip?
Yeah. I really like train travel,

and London is actually really close
to the rest of Europe,
but we have the sea in the way.

But we have the tunnel, so we can,
we can go to Paris, just for the day.

And I, it seems like maybe,
like it's not long enough

to just to go somewhere like Paris
for a day, but you can do it.

And I kind of like that idea
of just going from London to Paris
for the day and back on the train.
Sounds a bit crazy.
You'd need a really early train.

Really early train, really
late train back and lots of energy.
Yeah.
Yeah.

You wouldn't need to pay
for your accommodation.
Exactly.
Go up the Eiffel Tower, come back down, go home.
Perfect.

Let's recap the vocabulary we heard
during the conversation.

We had 'day trip', which means visiting
a place for just the day.

We heard 'thrill seeker', which
describes someone who takes part
in risky and exciting activities.

'Adventurous' means 'unusual
and exciting'.

A person can be adventurous,
or an experience or a day trip

can be adventurous,
like Neil's trip to Paris.

We used the word 'sightseeing',
which describes the activity

where you walk around looking
at famous buildings and monuments.

And that's it for this episode
of Real Easy English.

You can test what you've learnt
with the worksheet on our website,
bbclearningenglish.com.

And we'll be back
with more easy conversations soon.

And we want to hear from you to tell us
what you want to hear us talking about.

Send an email
to learningenglish@bbc.co.uk
We'd love to hear from you.
Thanks for joining us and see you soon.
Goodbye.
